Skip to contents
evfuse 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels

Development Guide

Source: DEVELOPMENT.md

Developer conventions and implementation notes for the evfuse package.

Key Conventions

  • Parameter-major ordering throughout (parameter index varies fastest): index (j-1)*L + i for param j at site i
  • Log-rho parameterization for optimization (ensures rho > 0)
  • Lambda = 300 km Wendland C4 (order-2) taper for bootstrap W
  • B = 500 bootstrap replicates
  • 20 multi-start random restarts from log-uniform[50, 5000] km
  • Prediction grid: filtered coastal points at 15 km spacing, lon -98 to -66, lat 24 to 46
  • Seeds: bootstrap (42), retry loop (101-105), multi-start (2026), return level simulation (123)
  • source_params abstraction: named list mapping source labels to parameter indices (e.g., list(NOAA = 1:3, ADCIRC = 4:6)); all core functions accept this via dat$source_params
  • Unicode labels in figures (μ₀, ξ, σ) require ragg::agg_png device in ggsave

Implementation Notes

  • extRemes::fevd returns observed information matrix directly (positive definite), not Hessian of negative log-likelihood. Use solve(fit$results$hessian) directly for vcov.
  • Delta method for log_sigma transformation: J = diag(1, 1/sigma, 1)
  • Rho optimized in log space: pack_params stores log(rho), unpack_params exponentiates. Gradient uses chain rule.
  • Analytic gradient contracts Q = V_inv - alpha*alpha^T with Omega_i into pxp H matrices. See Section 3.3 and Supporting Information Section S1 (Eqs S1-S4).
  • Sigma_obs built directly in observed space (never form full Lp x Lp).
  • nll/nll_grad share a cache keyed on parameter vector to avoid redundant Cholesky.
  • All core functions generalized via source_params; defaults match NOAA/ADCIRC case.
  • wendland2() implements Wendland C4 (psi_{2,2} from Furrer et al. 2006), not C2.

Nonstationary Extensions

Two nonstationary Stage 1 variants are available:

  • Linear time trend (fit_gev_detrended): fits μ(t) = μ₀ + μ₁(t − t_ref) at NOAA sites, discards μ₁ and passes (μ₀, log σ, ξ) to Stage 2. Used in the primary manuscript analysis.
  • Covariate sensitivity (fit_gev_ns): fits μ(t) = μ₀ + μ₁·covariate(t), retains μ₁ in θ̂ (4 columns). Supports asymmetric source_params (e.g., list(NOAA = 1:4, ADCIRC = 5:7) for p=7 model with 42 optimization parameters). Used for the forecasting extension.

Key implementation details for the covariate pipeline: - bootstrap_W_ns: Gamma uses union ordering (4 params/site); ADCIRC μ₁ positions are NA → cov() produces NA - embed_W: detects unobserved params via is.na() on W_tap diagonal, maps only observed entries - taper_W must use p = 4 (not default 3) for the nonstationary bootstrap - compute_return_levels_ns: 4-dim delta method gradient via chain rule

Data

  • Source: data(coast_data) or data-raw/combined_max.csv
  • 129 sites: 29 NOAA, 100 ADCIRC
  • 34-43 years of annual maxima per site

Development

  • Iterate with devtools::load_all() and devtools::test()
  • Run tests after every change (102 tests in tests/testthat/test-core.R)
  • R CMD check should pass with 0 errors, 0 notes (2 warnings from vignette inst/doc when pandoc is unavailable are expected)

Project Structure

R/                       Core package source (13 files)
scripts/                 Analysis and reproduction scripts
  run_nonstationary.R    Master script: reproduces all paper results
  simulation_study.R     Parameter recovery under non-co-location (Table 8)
  rmse_decomposition.R   RMSE decomposition by parameter and region (Table 3)
  baseline_comparisons.R Nearest-ADCIRC bias correction baselines (§5.1)
  run_trends.R           Trend diagnostics (trend map)
  plot_study_area.R      Study area map (Figure 1)
  gradient_benchmark.R   Analytic vs numerical gradient timing comparison
  supplementary/         Stationary pipeline, sensitivity, and exploratory analyses
data/                    Bundled package data (coast_data.rda)
data-raw/                Source data, data preparation, computed artifacts
tables/                  Summary tables (regenerated by run_nonstationary.R)
figures/                 Output figures (19 PNGs)
tests/testthat/          Unit tests (102 tests)
vignettes/               Tutorial vignette

On this page

Developed by Brian N. White, Brian Blanton, Rick Luettich, Richard L. Smith.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
